turns.user: Create a dataset named phase1_feed_raw using dataset_save with exactly these records: [{"title":"Renewable energy project expands","url":"https://example.com/energy-1","source":"Example News","published_at":"2026-05-20","snippet":"A new solar project expands renewable generation."},{"title":"Renewable energy project expands","url":"https://example.com/energy-1","source":"Example News","published_at":"2026-05-20","snippet":"Duplicate row for testing dedupe."},{"title":"Battery storage improves grid resilience","url":"https://example.com/energy-2","source":"Grid Weekly","published_at":"2026-05-21","snippet":"Battery storage is helping stabilize renewable-heavy grids."},{"title":"Football transfer rumours round-up","url":"https://example.com/sport-1","source":"Sport Daily","published_at":"2026-05-21","snippet":"A summary of football transfer rumours."},{"title":"Wind farm financing reaches new milestone","url":"https://example.com/energy-3","source":"Finance Wire","published_at":"2026-05-22","snippet":"Wind energy financing hit a new milestone this quarter."}]. After saving, call dataset_list and answer with the dataset list.
Use dataset_drop_where on phase1_feed_raw with predicate duplicate by url and save_as phase1_feed_deduped. Then call dataset_list and answer with the dataset list.
Call dataset_inspect on phase1_feed_deduped and tell me the schema and count.

Use dataset_filter on phase1_feed_deduped with save_as phase1_feed_relevant. Keep only items clearly about renewable energy, battery storage, wind energy, or grid resilience. Use fields [title, source, published_at, url, snippet]. After the tool call, answer with only the resulting dataset name.
Call dataset_get on phase1_feed_relevant with max_records 2 and fields [title, url]. Return the retrieved records.
Delete the datasets phase1_feed_raw, phase1_feed_deduped, and phase1_feed_relevant. Then answer with a short confirmation.
